advantages: Simple, fast linear method with mature library support (sklearn, NumPy SVD) and clear interpretability via loadings and explained variance.
Orthogonal components reduce redundancy and multicollinearity without manual feature pairing.
Unsupervised—works without labels, making it useful for EDA, compression, and preprocessing.
Explained variance ratio gives a principled criterion for choosing how many dimensions to keep.
disadvantages: Captures only linear structure; curved manifolds and complex non-linear relationships may require t-SNE, UMAP, or kernel methods.
Optimizes variance, not prediction or class separation—leading components may align with noise if noisy features have high variance.
Sensitive to scaling: covariance PCA overweights high-variance features unless you standardize deliberately.
Components are linear combinations of all inputs—sparse or interpretable single-feature axes are not guaranteed.
